title: C4OS Grill Question 002d: Browser Trust Claim
description: This question resolves whether C4OS keeps trusted-root containment as a product promise or introduces an explicitly unsafe Browser mode. Submit the form, then paste the generated response back into chat.

questions.label: How should C4OS describe Browser trust in MVP?
options: C4OS keeps trusted-root containment; Browser must not allow arbitrary file:// or any-page automation
C4OS adds an explicit Unsafe Browser mode that can access arbitrary file:// and approved-domain automation
C4OS weakens the overall product trust claim and allows Browser to escape trusted-root containment by default
Browser is removed from MVP until this can be made trusted-root-safe
questions.help: Recommended answer: add an explicit Unsafe Browser mode only if this capability is truly required. Do not silently weaken the whole product trust claim.

questions.label: If unsafe Browser exists, what user consent is required?
options: Per workspace opt-in with persistent warning and audit record
Per navigation approval for file:// and unknown domains
No special consent beyond opening Browser
questions.help: Recommended answer: per workspace opt-in plus per-domain automation approval. Arbitrary file:// should be a separate explicit action.

questions.label: Should this become an ADR before freeze?
options: Yes, Browser trust exception needs an ADR
No, keep it only in research notes
questions.help: Recommended answer: yes. This is hard to reverse, surprising, and a real trade-off against the trust model.

questions.label: Clarifications or exact product copy
questions.help: State how the product should explain this to users, especially if Browser can access arbitrary local files or automate arbitrary domains.
